skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Conduct thorough, constructive code reviews for quality and security. Use when reviewing pull requests, checking code quality, identifying bugs, or auditing security. Handles best practices, SOLID principles, security vulnerabilities, performance analysis, and testing coverage.
skill.summary: AI-powered code review assistant that analyzes pull requests for quality, security, and best practices
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 38 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains educational code examples showing vulnerable patterns (XSS, SQL injection, hardcoded secrets) as anti-patterns to identify during code reviews. External command references are Python code snippets in markdown, not actual execution. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate industry references (Google Code Review, OWASP). Environment access patterns demonstrate SECURE practices (using os.environ.get()). No actual security vulnerabilities present.

medium_findings.title: Educational Code Examples Misidentified
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged Python code examples in markdown as 'external commands' and 'XSS vulnerabilities'. These are educational examples showing anti-patterns to identify during code reviews.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged lines contain Python code examples in markdown code blocks demonstrating good/bad coding patterns. No actual command execution or vulnerabilities present.

low_findings.title: Reference Links to External Resources
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs are legitimate industry references (Google Code Review Guidelines, OWASP Top 10, Clean Code book). Standard educational resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to Google GitHub, OWASP.org, and Amazon - recognized industry-standard resources for code review best practices.

low_findings.title: Secure Environment Variable Usage Example
low_findings.description: Code example showing os.environ.get() for API key retrieval is a SECURE practice being taught, not a vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example explicitly shows GOOD practice (using environment variables) vs BAD practice (hardcoding). This teaches developers to avoid hardcoded secrets.

content.value_statement: This skill enables AI assistants to conduct professional code reviews by checking for bugs, security issues, code quality, and best practices. It provides structured feedback for pull requests.
actual_capabilities: Analyze pull requests for code quality issues
Check for performance bottlenecks and optimization opportunities
Verify test coverage and code documentation
Provide constructive feedback using the SBI (Situation-Behavior-Impact) model
Apply SOLID principles and clean code standards
limitations: Cannot execute code or run tests directly
Cannot access external services or APIs for live validation
May miss context-specific business logic issues
Relies on code being readable in the provided diff

use_cases.title: Review pull requests
use_cases.description: Get comprehensive feedback on code changes before merging, including security, quality, and performance suggestions

use_cases.title: Learn coding best practices
use_cases.description: Receive educational feedback explaining why certain patterns are problematic and how to improve them
use_cases.target_user: Junior developers learning to write better code

use_cases.title: Security audits
use_cases.description: Identify common security vulnerabilities like SQL injection, XSS, and hardcoded secrets in code changes

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Please review this pull request. Check for code quality, security issues, and best practices. Focus on the most important findings first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review of a small PR with a clear description

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a security audit of this code change. Look for SQL injection, XSS vulnerabilities, hardcoded secrets, authentication issues, and other security concerns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing code that handles user input or authentication

prompt_templates.title: Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this code for performance issues. Check for N+1 queries, inefficient algorithms, missing indexes, memory leaks, and resource management problems.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing database queries or data processing code

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a thorough code review covering: functionality, code quality, security, performance, testing, and documentation. Provide specific, actionable feedback with code examples where possible.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed review of a large feature PR

output_examples.output:
## Security Review

### Critical Issue: SQL Injection Vulnerability

**Location:** Line 2

**Problem:** The code uses f-string formatting to insert `user_id` directly into the SQL query. This allows attackers to inject malicious SQL code.

```python
# VULNERABLE
query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}"

# SECURE - Use parameterized query
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s"
cursor.execute(query, (user_id,))
```

**Recommendation:** Always use parameterized queries or an ORM to prevent SQL injection attacks.

best_practices: Provide specific, actionable feedback with code examples showing the preferred approach
Prioritize issues by severity: critical security issues first, then performance, then style
Acknowledge good code decisions alongside areas for improvement
Explain the reasoning behind each feedback point to help developers learn
anti_patterns: Making personal attacks or focusing on the person instead of the code
Vague feedback like 'this is bad' without explaining why or how to fix it
Ignoring good aspects of the code and only pointing out problems

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill provides general code review guidance applicable to any programming language. It includes examples in Python and JavaScript but the principles apply broadly.

faq.question: Can this skill run tests or linting tools?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance on what to check during code reviews. It cannot execute code, run tests, or use external tools. However, it lists recommended tools to use alongside the review.

faq.question: How does this skill handle large PRs?
faq.answer: For large PRs, prioritize the most important issues first. Focus on security vulnerabilities, potential bugs, and architectural concerns before style issues.

faq.question: Does this skill check for specific framework issues?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on general code quality and common security issues. Framework-specific concerns should be added based on your technology stack.

faq.question: Can this skill review multiple files?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide file paths or diffs for multiple files. Organize feedback by file or by category depending on what makes sense for the review.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for AI assistants like Claude or Codex to use during code review conversations. For automated checks, consider linters, security scanners, and testing in your CI pipeline.
